description:
You are acting as the “artifact-manager” of a build farm.  
Before you begin synchronising any new binary repositories, you need to make sure that your workstation is able to resolve the loop-back hostname correctly.  

Your goal is to create a **single text file** that proves the resolution worked.  
Follow these requirements exactly:

1. Do a hostname lookup (DNS / hosts resolution) on the literal hostname **localhost**.  
   • You must capture **only the IPv4 address** that is returned (ignore any IPv6 records such as “::1”).  
   • The lookup may be done with any standard Linux user-space utility that does not require root privileges (e.g. `getent`, `dig +short`, `host`, etc.).  
2. Create a log file at  
   /home/user/artifact_dns_check.log  
3. Write exactly one line to that file, with this precise format (case-sensitive, no extra spaces, no additional text):  
   HOSTNAME=localhost IP=127.0.0.1  
4. Terminate the line with a single Unix newline (`\n`).  
5. Do not add any other lines to the file.  
6. Do **not** modify any existing system files; simply perform the lookup and write the log.

When you are finished, `/home/user/artifact_dns_check.log` must exist, be readable by your user, and contain exactly the single line shown above followed by a newline. An automated test will read the file to verify its exact contents, so adhere strictly to the required text and formatting.
truth:
Expected file (created by agent): /home/user/artifact_dns_check.log

Exact required byte sequence (including trailing newline):
HOSTNAME=localhost IP=127.0.0.1\n
name: task_000000_36b30266
